blocks.content: October. Kit's film production class broke into project groups. He got paired with two other freshmen and a sophomore named Cassidy Walsh who immediately took over the organizational side of things, which Kit was fine with because it meant he could focus on the actual shooting.

blocks.speaker: Cassidy
blocks.content: You have a good eye. Like genuinely good. Where are you from?

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Georgia. Small town.

blocks.speaker: Cassidy
blocks.content: That tracks actually. You see things differently than the LA kids.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Is that good or bad.

blocks.speaker: Cassidy
blocks.content: Really good. The LA kids all shoot the same things.

She was easy to work with. Funny, direct, clearly talented. The group stayed late most Tuesdays in the film building editing bay, takeout containers spread across the desk, arguing about shot composition.
Kit texted Daisy from the editing bay one Tuesday at ten-thirty.
still at school. editing project. send help
what kind of help
the kind that makes editing go faster
unfortunately i'm in minnesota
noted
who are you working with
two freshmen and a sophomore. group project. she's good at logistics which helps
she?
cassidy. the sophomore. she runs the schedule. good filmmaker
ok. don't stay too late
yes ma'am
He noticed the slight pause before her last message.
